methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $5M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $110M - $1.8B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

data.narrative:
Endurance Energy is a seed-stage climate and energy company that has raised $5M+. The valuation analysis triangulates across a comparable universe of 9 private companies and 1 public company (LNZA) in the climate and energy sector, with peer reference points including TAE Technologies, Redwood Materials, Helion, Commonwealth Fusion Systems, and QuantumScape.

Two valuation methods were applied. The Stage-Progression method uses the $5M total raised as a baseline proxy and applies a 2.5x multiplier typical for seed-to-next-stage advancement, implying a $12M valuation. The Peer-Median method examines 4 same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables: Watershed ($1.8B Series A), Graphyte ($200M Series A), Fourth Power ($150M Series A), and Steady Energy ($110M Series A). The median of this peer set is $175M, with a range spanning $110M to $1.8B.

These two methods yield a triangulated valuation range of $11M (low) to $201M (high), with a median of $94M. The wide dispersion—particularly the $1.8B outlier—reflects material uncertainty in early-stage climate-tech valuation. The gap between stage-progression ($12M) and peer-median ($175M) suggests that comparable companies have achieved substantially greater scale or capital efficiency. Key uncertainties include the representativeness of the peer set (especially the Watershed outlier) and the target's progress relative to staged milestones.
